transcript: The movie begins by introducing Jigan Daisuke, a renowned thief and hitman armed with his trusty Wessel and Smith 19 revolver. Currently he is in Poland, where he faces off against a challenger at one night, but he manages to defeat the man. After this encounter, he immediately flies off to Mexico to attend shooting training. However, unlike usual Jijin's shots, which usually hit dead on, are now slightly off target by 0.5mm. Sensing something's amiss, he seeks help from a weapons expert who advises him to visit Japan's Degogai area. There he can find the best weapons specialist to fix his revolver. On the other side, we see Kyoko and Yuya, who are battered off for being beaten by gangsters. After they are trying to rescue Odo, a young girl held captive by local thugs. The following day, Jigen finally made it to Japan, and he hails a taxi straight to Digio, his hometown. Upon arrival, he meets Ruri to ask for directions. Soon after, he reaches his destination, only to find it overrun with gambling, brawling, and even prostitution. In the midst of his silent observation, a man named Kinjo approaches him. To Jigan's surprise, Kinjo is an old friend he hasn't seen in years. Qinjo then explains how the city has now become a place for outcasts. After a lengthy conversation, Kinjo reveals information about Chiharu Yaguchi, a weapons expert sought after by Jigen. According to the information, Yaguchi used to supply weapons to the Mafia boss who ruled the city. The next morning, Jigen heads to Yaguchi's place, based on Kinjo's information. However, instead of finding a weapons dealer, he discovers that the place has turned into a wall clock shop. To his further surprise, Yaguchi is an elderly woman. Nonetheless, Jigen remains wary and promptly presents his revolver, requesting repairs. The gun holds sentimental value for him, having been by his sides since his days as a thief and hitman. Despite his insistence, Yaguchi declines, citing retirement from the weapons trade long ago. Meanwhile, Kyoko and Yuya are having a heated argument, where Yuya wants to sell Odob to the mafia so he can live comfortably, but Kyoko refuses, having recently rescued her. The next day, Jigen reunites with an old friend who's a professional Go player. They both play and chat about wristwatches. Where Jigen admires his friend's fancy watch, which turns out to be a fake crafted by Yaguchi. Besides weapons, Yaguchi excels at making counterfeit watches, though she's never recognized in the industry. Instead, she sells them where her talent is valued. Moved by his friend's tale, Jigen visits Yaguchi's shop and orders a watch. While Yaguchi preparing the equipment, Odo enters the shop with a broken wristwatch. Recognizing it, Yaguchi asks where she got it. On the flip side, formidable leader of the city's mafia named Adele, confined to a wheelchair with damaged vocal cords and equipped with a voice assist device, is seen at the Yakuza headquarters. She is at the Yakuza headquarters because one of her henchmen is being held hostage by them. The Yakuza disapprove of Adele's illegal drug operation, since the drugs they produce come from children's blood and does not adhere to principles like the Yakuza. During a tense exchange with the Yakuza boss, Adele suddenly revealing her prosthetic leg that conceals a weapon, which she uses to eliminate her foes. After finishing them off, she instructs her right-hand man, Kawasima, to track down Odo, whose blood she needs for her drug production. Meanwhile, Jijin and Yaguchi are discussing Odo's wristwatch. It turns out to belong to Kyoko, which reminds Yaguchi of the incident that happened to Kyoko when she was pregnant. Kyoko had been forced by her boss to abort her pregnancy so she could keep working. In anger, she had wanted to buy a gun from Yaguchi to take revenge, but Yaguchi refused, instead offering free wristwatch repairs as a gesture of support. After telling the story, Yaguchi asks Jejin to accompany Odo home. Initially reluctant due to his dislike for children, Jigen changes his mind when Yaguchi offers to repair his weapon in exchange for protecting Odo. Putting his priority on having his revolver back in working order, Jigen agrees. Before they set off, Yaguchi provides Jijin with a pistol comparable to his magman combat for temporary use while his own gun is being fixed. During their journey with Odo, Jigen realizes she's unable to speak, so he gives her a book and pen to communicate. Following Odo's written instructions, they take a bus to Kyoko's house. Upon arrival, they discover Kyoko's lifeless body hanging gruesomely. It's evident she's been brutally murdered. Witnessing this, Odo recalls her own traumatic past, where her mother suffered a similar fate at the hands of her father. Before anyone else shows up, Jigen swiftly escorts Odo away. Shortly after, Kawasima and his crew emerge, tasked by Adele to capture Odo. However, they're all intimidated by Jigen's presence. Even Kawasima and his crew emerge, tasked by Adele to capture Odo. However, they're all intimidated by Jijin's presence. Even Kawasima acknowledges Jijin as a formidable killer not easily taken down. He then orders his men to keep a close eye on Jijin. Meanwhile, Adele is selling her own drug, boasting about its anti-aging properties derived from children's blood. She then showcases her youthful appearance at 52 as proof of its efficacy. Encouraging her clients to spread the word, she targets powerful figures like politicians, businessmen, and Mefi members, emphasizing the drug's ability to slow down their aging process will help them to prolong their influence. Late into the night, while Adele indulges in her product, Kawasima approaches with an elderly disguise. He informs her that Odo is with Jijin, making an immediate abduction impossible. Hearing that, Adele instructs Kawasima to maintain surveillance, awaiting an opportune moment to abduct the child. Meanwhile, Oda is asleep at Yaguchi's place, where the old woman comforts her during a nightmare. Yaguchi later informs Jigen that Odo can actually speak, having heard her muttering in her sleep. Hearing this, Jigen immediately realizes that Odo is suffering from aphonia, a mental disorder caused by trauma. Yaguchi suggests involving the police to protect Odo, but Jigen worries about their reliability. Meanwhile, Kinjo is confronted by Kawasima while dining, where he and his henchmen then point their guns and take Kinjo to a secluded place. There, Kawasima asks about Jigen's whereabouts, But Kinjo refuses to reveal Jigen's whereabouts, sensing Kawasima's ill intentions. The situation escalates until Kinjo rebels and is fatally shot. Kawasima then meets Ruri, revealing their forbidden love due to circumstances beyond their control. The following day, Jigen heads to the underground workshop in Yaguchi's shop to check on his pistol being repaired. He finds a letter from Yaguchi explaining that a specific component needs to be purchased before the repairs can proceed. Suddenly, he hears noises above and discovers Odo attempting to escape. Jigen tries to stop her, assuring her of their protection. As they try to leave for the watch shop, they are caught by the neighbors who think that Jigen is Yaguchi's nephew and Odo's biological father, who invite them to a meal. At the same time, Yaguchi goes to Degogai to gather information about Odo. Along the way, she encounters Ruri, whom she has known for a long time. During their conversation, Adele approaches, seeking to purchase weapons from Yaguchi due to her renowned reputation in the weapon trade. However, Yaguchi refuses to supply weapons for evil purposes, despite her past dealings with the Yakuza. On the other hand, Jijin is deeply troubled as he reads Odo's diary, because she writes down all of her traumatic past from parental abuse to her mother's murder and subsequent kidnapping, which led to her losing her voice. Moved by Odo's experiences, Jigen takes her with him to buy her a string to hang her diary, making it a cherished gift in her eyes. Shortly after, Jigen and Odo return to Yaguchi's shop, where they encounter a woman who is actually Kawasima in disguise, though they remain unaware at the time. As night falls, Jijin and Yaguchi discuss Odo's situation, but they don't have new information. Jigan then grows increasingly wary, suspecting Odo is being targeted. Determined to keep her safe, he plans to leave town with her. However, Kawasima's henchmen arrive at the shop. Without hesitation, Ji-Gan springs into action, swiftly overpowering them with his expertise, ensuring Odo's safety. However, Kawasima succeeds in kidnapping Odo, leaving Jijin to realize her true identity as the woman who visited the watch shop earlier. Thank you for watching. However, Kawasima succeeds in kidnapping Odo, leaving Jigen to realize her true identity as the woman who visited the watch shop earlier. In a desperate attempt to save Odo, Jigen draws his pistol, but he is stabbed before he can act. As Kawasima prepares to finish him off, Yaguchi rings the wall clock loudly, alerting the neighbors and thwarting Kawasima's attempt. The following day, Odo wakes up in a drug manufacturing facility, where her blood is being drained for serum production to maintain Adel's youthful appearance. With Odo's blood depleted, Adel must seek out other children to sustain her operations. Meanwhile, Jigen regains consciousness after being stabbed. Yaguchi urges him to leave the shop to rescue Odo and evade police capture. On the other hand, Yaguchi will surrender herself if the police come to investigate the death of Kawasima's henchmen in her shop. Additionally, she does this to redeem her sins of making weapons for evil people. Determined to save Odo and make things right, Jigen decides to confront the danger armed with Yaguchi's assembled pistol, promising to ensure Odo's safety and improve her life. That night, Jigen meets with an informant to gather information and orders bullets from the man. The informant then reveals that Adele, once an Interpol fugitive, now rules in Japan despite her disabilities. He also warns Jigen about Kawasima, an assassin And we'll be right back. informant then reveals that Adel, once an Interpol fugitive, now rules in Japan despite her disabilities. He also warns Jigen about Kawasima, an assassin with extraordinary abilities. Before Jigen leaves, the informant discloses that Kinjo has been killed and he was the one who betrayed him by selling information to Kawasima. Though Jigen doesn't blame the informant, he struggles to accept King Joe's death. After that conversation, he goes to Adol's headquarters to rescue Odo. Meanwhile, Adol's henchmen drain children's blood for an order while Adol reflects on her brutal past, where she has been brutal since childhood. And at that time, she even went so far as to kill her own mother. As Jijin enters Eidel's territory, but suddenly the alarm sound signaling his arrival. Hearing the sound, Kawasima rushes back to headquarters. However, Ruri looks worried about Kawasima's safety. But Kawasima promises to return safely. At the same time, while Eidel approaches Odo and notice how they have a lot in common, Kawasima comes to report Jigen's presence to Adol, who immediately orders his elimination, despite her earlier connection with Odo. Meanwhile, Jigen approaches Adol's headquarters, he casually navigating through the residence of Dago Gai, who lurk nearby. Unbeknownst to him, one thug aims to shoot him, but Jigen swiftly retaliates, sparking a confrontation. Despite being surrounded, Jigen efficiently dispatches his attackers with precision using Yaguchi's pistol. Employing cunning tactics, Jigen even triggers an explosion within Gas Tank, further overwhelming Adele's forces. G. Gam then infiltrates the headquarters, already facing off against a horde of mercenaries. Despite the overwhelming odds, Jigan skillfully avoids their attacks and gains the upper hand. As the mercenaries close in, Yaguchi arrives and shoots at the bus, signaling her support. She then hands over Jigen's pistol that has been repaired, and she is very confident that Jigen can save Odo. Upon hearing Yaguchi's encouragement, Jigen springs into action, swiftly emanates all the people, obstrusting him to ascend to the upper floor. There he confronts Kawasima, who lurks in the shadows and launches a knife attack. Using his wits, Jigen fires a shot into the air, illuminating the area and revealing Kawasima's position. In the ensuing struggle, Jigen emerges victorious, eliminating Kawasima. With Kawasima defeated, Jigen proceeds to the area where children's blood is being extracted. it. Horrified by the sight, he mercilessly killed Odo's henchmen. Amidst the chaos, he retrieves Odo's diary from the floor. Despite sustaining a gunshot wound to his leg, Jigen enters Odo's room to find Odo unconscious in her lap. To his astonishment, Odo suddenly speaks, surprising Jigen. Upon hearing Odo's voice, Jigen's resolve to rescue her strengthens. He vows never to let her suffer again. Adele then challenges Jigen to a high-speed shooting duel for the right to take Odo. In a swift exchange of gunfire, Jigen emerges victorious, proving his unrivaled skill. Ultimately, he saves Odo from the clutches of the nefarious mafia. After everything is over, Jigen, Odo, and Yaguchi reunite at the watch shop, where Yaguchi expresses her intention to adopt Odo, ensuring her safety, and for the first time, Odo finally smiles and laughs. The following day, Jigen decides to return to Europe to resume his career as a robber and hitman. Moral lesson from the story, when your gun's aim goes a little haywire, it's okay to ask an old lady who fixes clocks for help. But remember, even if your bullets miss, your kindness can still hit the target.
